explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey B, so I was looking at our budget review for next year. You know, last year's numbers are the anchor here – we can't really stray too far from what we did. Sales were 1.2 million, right? So I'm thinking we peg next year's projection at, like, 1.25 million tops. What do you think? Yeah, uh, 1.2 million sounds about right. Anchoring to that makes sense, I guess. But didn't we have that big client loss at the end? Still, if we're sticking to last year... expenses were around 900k? Marketing ate up 200k of that. Exactly, marketing was 200k last year, so let's keep it there. No need to inflate just because of some new trends or whatever. I mean, we tried boosting it before and it didn't pan out. Hey, speaking of, did you see that new coffee machine in the break room? Total waste if you ask me. Haha, yeah, the coffee machine – probably another 5k down the drain. But back to budget, salaries were 500k last year. With inflation and all, maybe nudge it to 520k? But nah, anchoring says stick to 500k, right? We don't want to look like we're overspending. Spot on. 500k it is. Um, R&D was only 100k last year – super low, but that's our baseline. Can't justify more without fresh data, and we're not doing that. Oh, tangent: remember that team-building retreat? Cost us 20k and everyone hated it.
